Probabilities are regressed toward 0.5 for more distant horizons to reflect increasing uncertainty.

The effective federal funds rate (EFFR) typically acts as a step function bound within the Fed's target range, experiencing only minor (1-2 basis points) noise driven by short-term liquidity, except during FOMC rate changes. Following a 25 bps rate cut on October 29, 2025, the EFFR settled at a stable 3.87% (within the new 3.75%-4.00% target range) and remained there through early November. The question asks whether the rate will 'have increased' compared to the November 9 baseline (which will be 3.87% based on preceding days) on three future resolution dates: Nov 16, Dec 9, and Feb 7. For the immediate horizons (Nov 16, Dec 9), there are no scheduled FOMC meetings, meaning rate changes would rely solely on upward intra-range drift or technical adjustments (like repo/IORB tweaks). The likelihood of a purely random upward drift strictly greater than the 3.87% baseline is small, hence a low initial probability. Over the longer horizon (Feb 7, 2026), the FOMC will have had two meetings (Dec 2025, Jan 2026). Current market expectations (via CME FedWatch) heavily favor continued rate cuts or holds, pushing the most likely expected value downward, not upward. Thus, the expected economic probability of an increase drops further. However, per the strict instructions to increase uncertainty with the forecast horizon and push distant probabilities closer to 0.5, the assigned probabilities are structured to grow monotonically, reflecting the non-linear tail risks of unexpected inflation shocks or structural regime shifts in the longer term.

The baseline value as of Nov 9, 2025 is 0.40%. Savings deposit rates are sticky but generally move in tandem with the Federal Funds rate. Recent market consensus (as of late October 2025) strongly expects the Federal Reserve to continue cutting its policy rate by 25 basis points at the October and December meetings, aiming for around 3.6% by year-end 2025 and lower in 2026. Because the Fed is in an active easing cycle, banks face no competitive or economic pressure to raise the interest rates they pay on savings accounts. In fact, many institutions lower their yields immediately after a Fed rate cut. Consequently, the national average savings rate (which has been flat at 0.40% for the past three months following a slight recovery from a June drop) is highly likely to remain flat or decrease as the latest and upcoming Federal Reserve cuts take effect in the banking system. Short-term bounces upward are unlikely but possible due to noise in the survey/weightings, so the probability is kept modestly above zero, increasing slightly for the later resolution date due to inherent forecasting uncertainty over longer horizons.

Because the resolution criteria explicitly state that "export controls must apply broadly, and must not be targeted or limited to a single country or entity," the May directives aimed heavily at China and Huawei, in addition to largely being "guidance" enforcing existing General Prohibitions (GP 10), are unlikely to qualify as "broad application of new or expanded controls." While BIS indicated they will issue replacement rules, as of the knowledge cutoff (November 2025), no new broad comprehensive rules on AI chips, cloud access, or model weights have been finalized with an effective date in 2025 that meet the required "broad application" without singling out a specific country/entity. The probability is therefore kept relatively low, as the primary 2025 action ended up being a rescission of regulations rather than a broad expansion.

The major budget reconciliation bill ("One, Big, Beautiful Bill Act") was enacted on July 4, 2025. Instead, it maintained the domestic content requirement as a bonus "adder" and merely aligned the 48E requirements with the existing 45Y structure. There is no evidence of any other federal bill being proposed or passed before the end of 2025 that makes this specific change. Given the recent passage of the reconciliation bill, the window for such a major structural change in 2025 has essentially closed.

Given these enormous hurdles, his chances are very slim, noticeably lower than the initial Polymarket price of 11% (which likely reflected hopes of Dati being legally disqualified).

The question asks whether Kateryna Lagno's FIDE ranking on three resolution dates will be "high or higher than their ranking on 2025-11-09" (which is 9th).
Note: Wait, FIDE rank 1 is "higher" than FIDE rank 9. The phrasing "high or higher than their ranking" typically means a smaller number (e.g., 8, 7, 6, 1) or equal (9).

For the 2025-11-16 (actually 2024-11-16) date: The FIDE November list is published on Nov 1st and lasts until Dec 1st. Live ratings do NOT affect the official FIDE list until the next month's publication. Thus, her ranking on Nov 16th will unequivocally be the same as it was on Nov 9th, which is 9. Therefore, probability 1 is extremely close to 1 (0.98).

For the remaining dates (Dec 9th, Feb 7th): Lagno played in the Shymkent Women's Grand Prix from Oct 30 to Nov 8. She struggled mightily, starting -2 (e.g. 2 losses). Although she won one game against Assaubayeva in round 6 and drew the rest, her overall score was quite poor (likely -1 or -2 for the event). 
Before this event, she was 2517 (9th), Assaubayeva was 2513 (10th), and Divya Deshmukh was 2505 (11th). Both Assaubayeva and Divya Deshmukh played in Shymkent. Assaubayeva finished tied for 3rd and Divya also won games (e.g., beat Munguntuul and Humpy). It is highly probable that Lagno lost significant rating points (dropping below 2510) and was overtaken by Assaubayeva and possibly Divya Deshmukh on the live list. The impact of this will register on the Dec 1st FIDE list, dropping Lagno to 10th or 11th (a lower ranking). Since she dropped rank, she will not be 9th or better. 

I assign a 30% chance that she remains 9th or better on Dec 9th (perhaps Anna Muzychuk or someone ahead of her lost even more points). For Feb 7, it's slightly more uncertain as she might play more events and regain the ranking, but she's not scheduled to play heavily compared to younger active players, so 35% probability.

Sepsis is a medical syndrome that represents the body's dysregulated immune response to an infection, rather than an infection by a single specific pathogen. Because of this, creating a 'sepsis vaccine' is scientifically nonsensical in the traditional sense, though there are vaccines for pathogens that frequently cause sepsis (like pneumococcus or meningococcus) and vaccines in early development for neonatal sepsis caused by specific bacteria like E. coli and K. pneumoniae. However, the Wikipedia page 'List of infectious diseases' specifically requires a vaccine for the entry itself. Given the 'No' status as of late October 2025 and the biological reality of Sepsis, it is virtually impossible that an approved vaccine for Sepsis will emerge or that Wikipedia's consensus will incorrectly list one as 'Yes' over the extremely short forecast horizon of 1 to 3 months. To account for any slight possibility of anomalous Wikipedia edits (e.g., vandalism or misinterpretation of vaccine candidates that go uncorrected), the probabilities are set near the minimum allowed, slightly increasing with time to reflect the growing, albeit very tiny, horizon uncertainty.

Therefore, the chance of a competitor breaking this gruelling 1500m LC record in an off-season or early-season meet before February 2026 is vanishingly small. The only other risk would be a delayed doping disqualification from Paris 2024, but after over a year this is also highly improbable. To satisfy the requirement for uncertainty increasing over time and remaining within the 0.05–0.95 bounds, I predict 0.95, 0.94, and 0.92.

Liu Zige's 200m butterfly long course record of 2:01.81 has stood since the 2009 supersuit era. Summer McIntosh came extremely close recently, clocking 2:01.99 at the World Championships in July 2025. While she is a massive threat to the record, the likelihood of it falling between November 2025 and February 2026 is relatively low. The winter swimming calendar is largely dominated by short course events (like the World Cup series) or short course yards for US-based training. Major long course competitions usually don't take place until the spring (e.g., national trials in March/April/May). Even if someone swims at a long course meet like the December US Open or a Jan/Feb TYR Pro Swim Series, swimmers are typically not rested or peaked in the middle of their training blocks, making a world record in a gruelling event like the 200m butterfly quite rare. Therefore, the record is highly likely to survive the winter. I am decaying the probability gradually from 0.98 (1 week out) to 0.88 (3 months out) to account for the possibility of an unexpected breakthrough.

The Wikipedia page "List of infectious diseases" currently leaves the "Vaccine(s)" column blank for Gonorrhea (as of Oct 2025). The only vaccine specifically targeting Gonorrhea in active clinical trials is GSK's NgG, which is only in Phase 2 with a readout expected in 2025. This means that formal development and approval are years away. While the meningitis vaccine Bexsero (4CMenB) provides some cross-protection and is being used off-label in the UK (since May 2025), it has not prompted Wikipedia editors to add it as a Gonorrhea vaccine in the several months since. Thus, the likelihood of a newly approved vaccine being developed and listed on this Wikipedia page, or the page being revised to include Bexsero, by February 2026 is extremely low. Evaluated probabilities remain near the bottom boundary.

Based on the Wikipedia page for FIDE rankings as of the November 9, 2025 knowledge cutoff, Wesley So is ranked 8th in the world (with a rating of 2764 on the November 1 list). FIDE updates its official classical rating lists once a month, typically on the 1st. 

1. **2025-11-16:** Because official FIDE rankings only update monthly, the November 1 list will still be the current active list on November 16. Therefore, his ranking on November 16 will identically be 8, which satisfies the condition of being "as high or higher" than his November 9 ranking (which is also 8). The probability of YES is near certainty (0.95).

2. **2025-12-09:** This date will rely on the December 1 FIDE list. Wesley So is securely in 8th place, but the rating band is extremely crowded: 4 players (Keymer, Giri, Erigaisi, Praggnanandhaa) reside just 4–9 points above him, while Gukesh and Firouzja reside just 1–2 points below him. Because there are more vulnerable players directly above him who could drop points than directly below him, and Wesley So is known for a solid playing style in classical chess, he is reasonably favored to maintain a top-8 spot. Probability: 0.65.

3. **2026-02-07:** This will use the February 1, 2026 list. Due to the further time horizon and the inclusion of potentially highly-volatile super-tournaments in January (which could randomly shift the closely-packed players), my uncertainty increases significantly. Consequently, the prediction drifts downward closer to the 0.5 baseline. Probability: 0.55.

The FIDE Elo rating updates on the 1st of every month. The rating on November 9, 2025 will simply be the November 1 rating, which according to Wikipedia is 2569. To be at least 1% higher, Lei Tingjie's rating must reach approximately 2595. 
1. For 2025-11-16, the rating on Wikipedia will still reflect the November 1 list, so it will be exactly equal to the rating on November 9 (no change is possible). The probability is practically 0 (I will use 0.02, the minimum allowed).
2. For 2025-12-09, her rating would need to jump by 26 points by the December 1 update. At the elite 2570 level, gaining 26 points in a single month requires an incredibly rare massive overperformance (+6 or +7 score against strong opponents), and there are no major classical tournaments scheduled for her in November that could offer such a swing. The base rule for 1% Elo increase is extremely low (0.007). Probability is 0.02.
3. For 2026-02-07, covering the January and February FIDE updates, the timeline is slightly longer but reaching 2595 remains remarkably difficult, requiring sustained dominance over several months. Factoring in her recent withdrawal from the FIDE Women's Grand Prix, her tournament volume might be low. I am setting it slightly higher at 0.03 to reflect uncertainty over a longer time horizon.

The current status for a Trachoma vaccine on Wikipedia is 'No' as of late October 2025. Web search indicates that while there are promising vaccine candidates in development for Chlamydia trachomatis (which causes Trachoma), the most advanced ones recently reported were only completing Phase 1 clinical trials (e.g., CTH522 in 2024). A March 2025 paper details plans for 'clinical development' models, confirming no vaccine is imminently ready. Vaccine development takes several years through Phases 2 and 3 and regulatory approval. It is effectively impossible for a vaccine to be fully developed and recognized by Wikipedia within the next 3 months (by February 2026). The probabilities are set near the minimum allowable bound of 0.02 and slightly increase with time to account for the expanding forecast horizon.

FIDE classical ratings are published only once a month, typically on the 1st of the month. Therefore, on the first resolution date (2025-11-16), the rating on Wikipedia will identically mirror the value on 2025-11-09, making a 1% increase impossible. Thus, the first date resolves NO (probability set to minimum allowed, 0.02). 

For the subsequent dates (2025-12-09 and 2026-02-07), Caruana would need to increase his rating by 1% from the Nov 2025 baseline. Wikipedia's "FIDE rankings" page from October 31 shows his updated Nov 1 rating is 2795. A 1% increase means he would have to reach 2823. Gaining 28 rating points over a few months at the K=10 level for a top 3 player in the world requires an extraordinarily dominant winning streak in classical chess (something on the order of an ~2950 performance rating across dozens of games). Caruana has not been above 2820 in recent years. Because it is highly improbable for any player at 2795 to gain 28 Elo over such a short horizon—let alone during a winter period with relatively few classical super-tournaments outside of Tata Steel—the probabilities for the December and February dates remain very close to the 0.007 base rate and heavily bound near the 0.02 minimum threshold.

Magnus Carlsen has maintained the FIDE world number 1 ranking continuously for over 13 years (since 2011). His current rating on the November 1st, 2025 list is 2839, giving him a 26-point lead over the world number 2, Hikaru Nakamura (2813). In top-level classical chess, a 26-point deficit is immense and incredibly difficult to bridge over a 1-to-3 month period. 

Furthermore, Magnus Carlsen has skipped the FIDE Chess World Cup in November 2025, meaning his classical rating is largely frozen and insulated from massive drops. Attempting to bypass him would require Nakamura (or another player) to achieve an absurdly high plus-score against elite competition, or grind open tournaments (where expected points gained per win are less than 1, and drawing a single game loses 4-5 points, neutralizing any progress). 

Wikipedia updates its FIDE rankings monthly (around the 1st of the month). Therefore:
1) November 16, 2025: The Wikipedia table will still reflect the Nov 1st rankings. The outcome is virtually guaranteed.
2) December 9, 2025: The December 1st list will reflect November results. It is highly unlikely anyone bridges the 26-point deficit during this time, considering the rating system mechanics in place.
3) February 7, 2026: This incorporates results through January 2026 (including Tata Steel). Even if Carlsen plays and performs sub-optimally, the 26-point cushion provides a formidable margin of safety. Probabilities are structured to slightly lower for distant horizons.

All three leading norovirus vaccine candidates are far from approval. Moderna's Phase 3 candidate experienced lower-than-expected case accruals and is extending its trial through the 2025-2026 Northern Hemisphere season, pushing efficacy data into 2026 at the earliest. HilleVax's advanced candidate failed its Phase 2b trial in infants in mid-2024. Vaxart's oral pill candidate is only passing Phase 1. Thus, there is zero credible prospect of a norovirus vaccine being approved and added to the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' by February 2026. The only minor risk components are Wikipedia vandalism / premature editing, or a completely unexpected regulatory approval for a niche adult candidate internationally. Given the short time horizons (Nov 16, 2025; Dec 9, 2025; Feb 7, 2026), the probability remains extremely low.
